rules.title: Repository security policy is missing
rules.description: Reporters may disclose vulnerabilities publicly or abandon reports when no supported private route is documented.
rules.safe_remediation: Add SECURITY.md with supported versions, a private reporting route, response expectations, and a warning not to disclose secrets publicly.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS boundary is missing
rules.description: Repository control-plane files can be changed without an explicit ownership boundary.
rules.safe_remediation: Add real owners for workflows, issue templates, the security policy, and CODEOWNERS itself, then enforce owner review separately where appropriate.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS does not explicitly cover .github
rules.description: A broad ownership rule can be weakened without an explicit review boundary for workflows and repository automation.
rules.safe_remediation: Add a specific /.github/ ownership rule naming an eligible, active owner and verify that repository rules enforce the intended review.

rules.title: Structured issue form is missing
rules.description: Unstructured reports can omit reproduction evidence and increase maintainer triage cost.
rules.safe_remediation: Add a non-destructive YAML issue form requesting reproduction steps, expected behavior, and relevant versions, and route vulnerabilities privately.

rules.title: Machine-readable dependency update policy is missing
rules.description: Pinned Actions and packages can remain on vulnerable versions when no update mechanism or review cadence is configured.
rules.safe_remediation: Configure Dependabot or Renovate for the repository's real ecosystems and review update pull requests before merging.

rules.title: Branch-protection expectations are not documented locally
rules.description: Maintainers cannot compare the intended review and merge boundary with the external repository configuration.
rules.safe_remediation: Document expected reviews, checks, force-push restrictions, bypass owners, and emergency procedure, then verify actual settings with read-only API access.

rules.title: Workflow has no top-level token boundary
rules.description: A future job can inherit broader repository-default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions than intended.
rules.safe_remediation: Add permissions: {} at workflow level and grant only required permissions per job, then test the workflow before merging.

rules.title: Workflow grants write-all token permissions
rules.description: A compromised Action or command can use unnecessary write authority to modify sensitive repository resources.
rules.safe_remediation: Replace write-all with an empty default and enumerate only the exact job-level write permissions required.

rules.title: GitHub Action is not pinned to a full commit SHA
rules.description: A mutable tag or branch can move to compromised code without a reviewed workflow change.
rules.safe_remediation: Resolve the reviewed release to a full commit SHA, retain the release tag in a comment, verify provenance, and automate reviewed updates.

rules.title: Privileged event checks out attacker-influenced code
rules.description: A fork or attacker-controlled event can influence code executed in a context with base-repository authority.
rules.safe_remediation: Separate metadata handling from code execution and run contributor code only under pull_request with read-only permissions and no secrets.

rules.title: Untrusted input can reach secrets or write authority
rules.description: Attacker-controlled pull-request content can plausibly reach credentials or repository-changing authority.
rules.safe_remediation: Remove secrets and writes from the untrusted path, isolate contributor-code execution, and pass only validated artifacts or metadata to privileged stages.

rules.title: Checkout may persist a write-capable token
rules.description: A later command or compromised build step can recover a persisted credential and use its repository authority.
rules.safe_remediation: Set persist-credentials: false unless authenticated Git is required, then isolate that operation in a narrow least-privilege job.

rules.title: Destructive moderation is enabled
rules.description: A false positive or configuration mistake can close, lock, or delete legitimate contributor work without human review.
rules.safe_remediation: Switch to report-only mode, require human review, measure false positives, and publish an appeal path before enabling enforcement.

rules.title: Identity or history proxy is used for contributor risk
rules.description: A legitimate newcomer can be penalized for identity or history characteristics unrelated to the submitted change.
rules.safe_remediation: Disable the proxy and evaluate reproducibility, scope, tests, policy compliance, and contributor responsiveness instead.

rules.title: Destructive moderation has no discoverable appeal path
rules.description: A moderation false positive can become permanent when contributors have no documented route to human reconsideration.
rules.safe_remediation: Document the appeal channel, human owner, response expectation, reopening process, and emergency disable path.
